title: Outreach Memo
subtitle: A concise Iowa summary for first conversations
summary: A short University of Iowa-facing memo for internal forwarding that summarizes Steve's immediate research value, the PI or lab base opportunity, and the longer infrastructure upside.
synopsis: A concise bridge for faculty, grants staff, development staff, and other internal stakeholders who need the opportunity explained quickly. It leads with practical research data value, names the PI or lab base opportunity, and points readers toward the supporting material that matches their role.

slides.title: University of Iowa Outreach Memo
slides.summary: The shortest version of the case is simple: Steve can help Iowa research teams now, and a PI or lab base would make that value easier to use, support, and grow.
bullets: A PI or lab base as the institutional base
slides.speech: The University of Iowa opportunity can be stated simply. Steve can help research teams now with DMS Plans, workflow implementation, validation, documentation, and repository delivery. A PI, lab, or unit relationship would make that work easier to use, support, and grow while keeping longer-term SignWriting stewardship as a separate institutional question.
slides.notes: A busy reader should understand the practical value before hearing the larger infrastructure story.
slides.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Practical Research Support Now] --> B[PI Or Lab Base]
  B --> C[Support And Grants]
  B --> D[Longer-Term SignWriting Infrastructure]

slides.title: Why Now
slides.summary: The timing matters because the work is already active, the need is current, and a durable next structure is more credible when built now rather than after momentum is lost.
bullets: A near-term PI path exists now
The next step is small but strategically important
slides.speech: This moment matters because the work already has real value but still lacks a durable institutional structure. A near-term PI path now makes the next step much more credible. The point is not to solve everything at once. The point is to secure the right first structure.
slides.notes: Keep the urgency grounded in continuity, not alarm.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: A strategic timing slide showing a narrow opening for action leading to a more stable long-term path, clean academic style
slides.image_prompt_abstract: A brief opening in a wall leading to a brighter structured corridor

slides.title: Why Steve Matters Now
slides.summary: Steve's work is immediately useful because he can help with DMS Plans, workflow implementation, data shaping, code, validation, documentation, and repository delivery.
slides.speech: Steve matters now because he can create immediate practical value. He can help with Data Management and Sharing Plans, workflow implementation, code and data shaping, validation, documentation, and the difficult path from project systems to repository delivery. That makes him useful to real projects immediately, not only to future plans.
slides.notes: This is the slide that turns abstract interest into a concrete value case.
slides.mermaid:
graph LR
  A[Steve Slevinski] --> B[DMS Plan Support]
  A --> C[Workflow And Code]
  A --> D[Repository Delivery]

slides.title: The Larger Opportunity
slides.summary: The larger infrastructure opportunity is real because Sutton SignWriting already depends on public technical assets that need durable stewardship.
bullets: Continuity still depends on informal structure
Stronger when attached to visible research value
slides.speech: There is a larger opportunity around Sutton SignWriting as shared research infrastructure. That opportunity becomes more credible when it is attached to visible research value, a real institutional base, and collaborators who can help the work grow responsibly.
slides.notes: Keep the infrastructure story present but secondary to immediate usefulness.
slides.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Global Use] --> B[Continuity Risk]
  B --> C[Host Structure]
  C --> D[Funded Infrastructure]

slides.title: Recommended Next Conversations
slides.summary: The next step should match the reader: practical project support, PI or lab base discussion, infrastructure development, or philanthropic interest.
bullets: Researchers who need DMS and repository help -> Research Team Value
PI and faculty collaborators -> PI or Lab Base
Development and gift conversations -> Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy
slides.speech: The next conversations should be practical. Some readers need the PI or lab base case. Some need the immediate research-service case. Others need the long-term infrastructure or development materials. The point is to make the next conversation concrete.
slides.notes: Close with practical next actions and keep the language forwardable.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: Decision slide showing four next conversations: PI, DMS support, infrastructure, and grants/development
slides.image_prompt_abstract: One central hub branching into four purposeful next-step paths
